stories.title: Samantha Swan Officially Signs with Texas — 3-Peat Roster Takes Shape
stories.angle: After visiting Texas on June 10, ASU graduate transfer catcher Samantha Swan officially signed with the two-time defending national champions. First-Team All-Big 12 (.383 BA / 12 HR / 55 RBI / .917 career OPS), Swan fills Texas's most urgent roster hole. The Georgetown, TX native is returning home and potentially paves the way for fellow Georgetown-area prospect Isa Torres to follow.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Texas just landed the portal's No. 1 catcher.

Samantha Swan — All-Big 12, .383 BA, 12 HR, 55 RBI, .917 career OPS — is officially a Longhorn.

The Georgetown, TX native is coming home. The 3-peat roster is taking shape.

#HookEm #SoftballTwitter

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Fun fact: Samantha Swan went 4-for-10 with a HR and 5 RBI against Texas in the 2026 Super Regional.

Now she's playing for them.

That's the portal era in one sentence. #HookEm #SoftballTwitter

article.headline: Samantha Swan Signs with Texas: Two-Time Champs Fill Biggest Portal Need

---

research.facts:
Samantha Swan Signs with Texas
**Sources:**
- On3.com: "Arizona State catcher Samantha Swan commits to Texas" — Swan signed with Texas two days after entering the portal; First-Team All-Big 12; .383/.700/.450 slashline; 69 hits, 55 RBI, 12 HR, 21 doubles in 62 games; went 4-10 vs. Texas in Super Regional with 1 HR and 5 RBI
- Texas Athletics (texaslonghorns.com): "Softball signs Arizona State transfer Samantha Swan" — official program announcement
- 247Sports: "Texas softball signs Arizona State senior transfer catcher Samantha Swan" — ranked No. 9 overall, No. 1 catcher in portal by Softball America; one year eligibility; from Georgetown, TX; immediately eligible
- SI.com: "Texas Softball Lands All-Big 12 Catcher Out of Transfer Portal" — fills most urgent roster hole after Reese Atwood graduated; .917 career OPS
- Confidence: HIGH (multiple independent sources confirming signing)
**Freshness:** Signed June 10, 2026. Covered as "commitment watch" on June 11 pipeline — confirmed signing is new angle for June 12.

stories.title: Torres Watch — Day 5, Decision Window Narrows
stories.angle: D1 Softball Player of the Year Isa Torres (.530 BA / .978 SLG / .591 OBP / 78 runs) is now 5 days into the 15-day portal window with no commitment. Texas and Texas A&M remain the frontrunners. Torres is from Georgetown, TX — 30 minutes from Austin. With Swan signed, Texas's roster is becoming increasingly attractive. Portal closes June 22.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Day 5. Still no commitment from Isa Torres.

.530 BA. .978 SLG. .591 OBP. D1 Player of the Year.

Texas and Texas A&M are waiting. The portal closes June 22.

10 days left. ⏳ #SoftballTwitter

article.headline: The Torres Watch: Day 5, Ten Days Left, and the Softball World Still Waiting

**Article Hero (1200x630)**

---

research.facts:
Torres Watch — Day 5
**Sources:**
- Fox Sports / Washington Post: Torres entered portal June 8. D1 Softball Player of the Year, ACC Player of the Year. School records: .530 BA, .978 SLG, .591 OBP, 78 runs. NCAA record: hit in 16 consecutive at-bats.
- SI.com "Texas Softball Linked to Star Infielder in Transfer Portal" — Texas primary destination reported
- Yahoo Sports / SI.com transfer rankings: Torres ranked No. 2 behind Beachum by Softball America
- Georgetown, TX connection (30 min from Austin campus) — same hometown as Swan
- Portal closes June 22 (15-day window from June 8)
- No commitment reported as of June 12 (Day 5)
- Confidence: HIGH on stats (multiple sources), MEDIUM on specific destination (reported, not confirmed)
**Freshness:** Day 5 of portal window. Window narrows — 10 days remain.

stories.title: Jaysoni Beachum — Day 5 Uncommitted, Nation's No. 1 Transfer Still Available
stories.angle: Beachum (.417 BA / 16 HR / 66 RBI / .549 OBP) enters Day 5 without a commitment. No. 1-ranked transfer by Softball America. Three-year Seminole: 37 career HRs, 180 RBIs, 202 hits. NFCA 2024 Freshman of the Year, 2026 Second-Team All-American. Every power program in the country is watching.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
No. 1 portal transfer. Still available.

Jaysoni Beachum — .417 BA, 16 HR, 66 RBI this season. Career: 37 HRs, 180 RBIs, 202 hits in 3 seasons at FSU.

Day 5. No commitment. The bidding war is real. #SoftballTwitter

article.headline: The Bidding War Continues: Jaysoni Beachum, Day 5 Uncommitted, Nation's No. 1 Transfer

research.facts:
Jaysoni Beachum — Day 5
**Sources:**
- SI.com: "Florida State Softball All-American Enters Transfer Portal" — Beachum entered portal June 2. Three-year starter. NFCA 2024 Freshman of the Year. 2026 Second-Team All-American.
- 247Sports: "Florida State junior third baseman Jaysoni Beachum shares intentions to enter the NCAA Transfer Portal" — originally entered portal June 2 (before the window opened? clarify: this was before the June 8 window — she may have entered the general portal, not the 15-day window)
- SI.com transfer rankings: Beachum ranked No. 1 transfer by Softball America
- Stats (career 3 seasons): .375 BA / 37 HR / 35 2B / 180 RBI / 158 runs / 202 hits. First Seminole, first ACC athlete to win NFCA Division I Freshman of the Year.
- 2026 season: .417 BA / 16 HR / 66 RBI / .549 OBP / 54 walks
- No commitment announced as of June 12
- Confidence: HIGH on stats (multiple sources confirm)
**Freshness:** Still uncommitted Day 5. Major prize with every program watching.

stories.title: Portal Roundup — Plumlee to Alabama, Polar Still Available at Midpoint
stories.angle: Kailey Plumlee (Tennessee LHP, 4 years eligible) commits to Alabama — first LHP for Crimson Tide since 2024. Alabama's pitching staff (1.57 ERA, 2nd nationally) just got deeper. Meanwhile, Purdue's Moriah Polar (.555 BA / 101 hits / 2nd-team All-American) is on Day 5 with no commitment as the portal window approaches its midpoint.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Portal update:

✅ Kailey Plumlee (Tennessee LHP, 4 years eligible) commits to Alabama — first LHP for the Tide since 2024.

⏳ Moriah Polar (.555 BA, 101 hits, 2nd-team All-American) is still available on Day 5.

#SoftballTwitter

article.headline: Portal Roundup: Alabama Lands Plumlee, Polar Still Available as Window Hits Midpoint

research.facts:
Portal Roundup — Plumlee to Alabama + Polar
**Sources:**
- Rocky Top Insider: "Tennessee Softball Transfer Pitcher Commits to Alabama Through Portal" — Kailey Plumlee committed June 10. Redshirt freshman. From Carthage, TN. 4 years eligibility remaining. First LHP for Alabama since Lauren Esman in 2024. Alabama staff: Jocelyn Briski anchors (SEC POY, 1.57 ERA nationally 2nd).
- On3.com: "All-American outfielder Moriah Polar to enter transfer portal" — entered June 8. .555 BA / 101 hits / 218 career hits / 103 RBIs / 137 runs at Purdue. 2nd-team All-American. One year eligibility.
- Purdue Exponent: confirms Polar entered portal; nation's leading hitter in 2026
- No Polar commitment announced as of June 12
- Confidence: HIGH on Plumlee commitment (confirmed official); HIGH on Polar stats (multiple sources)
**Freshness:** Plumlee committed June 10 (new reporting for today). Polar on Day 5 of window with no commitment.

stories.title: Texas Coaching Search — White Races the Portal to Fill Two Open Spots
stories.angle: Mike White needs to hire two coaches before the 2027 season while simultaneously recruiting from the portal. Zaleski (associate HC) and Earleywine (OC who engineered 99 HRs, Katie Stewart's 31-HR season) are both gone. Texas landed Swan quickly — but the staff rebuild is just beginning. NFCA coaching transactions are active.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Texas won back-to-back titles with Zaleski and Earleywine on staff.

Both are gone.

Mike White now has 2 open coaching spots, a portal window closing in 10 days, and a 3-peat to chase.

No pressure. #HookEm #SoftballTwitter

article.headline: Two Open Seats, One Championship Window: Texas Races to Rebuild Its Coaching Staff

research.facts:
Texas Coaching Search
**Sources:**
- Yahoo Sports: "Texas softball: Assistant coaches Karen Zaleski and Ehren Earleywine leaving NCAA champs" — both leaving after two-time championship run
- SI.com: "Texas Softball Loses Second Piece of National Championship Winning Coaching Staff" — Earleywine departs for Tennessee; Zaleski departure reported
- NFCA transactions page lists coaching transactions; Colorado State (Gascoigne from Northwestern, June 8), Tulsa (DJ Gasso from Arkansas), Nebraska (Jordy Frahm) also active
- Texas Athletics coaches page: Mike White remains HC
- Two open spots: associate HC (Zaleski role) + offensive coordinator (Earleywine role)
- Earleywine engineered 99 team HRs in 2026, Katie Stewart's 31-HR record
- Confidence: HIGH on departures (official Texas Athletics, multiple sources); MEDIUM on specific replacement searches (no candidates reported yet)
**Freshness:** Follow-up to June 10/11 coaching departure stories. No replacements named yet.
